{{Short description|none}}
{{About|the men's team|the women's team|Somalia women's national football team}}
{{Use dmy dates|date=November 2019}}
{{Infobox national football team
| Name               = Somalia
| Nickname           = The Ocean Stars
| Badge              = Somali Football Federation logo.svg
| Badge_size         = 185px
| FIFA Trigramme     = SOM
| Association        = [[Somali Football Federation]] (SFF)<br>({{lang|so|Xiriirka Soomaaliyeed ee Kubbadda Cagta}})
| Sub-confederation  = [[CECAFA]]<br />(Central & East Africa)
| Confederation      = [[Confederation of African Football|CAF]] (Africa)
| Coach              = [[Yusuf Ali Nur]]
| Captain            = [[Abdulsamed Abdullahi]]
| Most caps          = [[Omar Abdulkadir]] (31)
| Top scorer         = [[Rashed saeed]] (8) <br> [[Khalid saeed]] (13)
| Home Stadium       = [[Mogadishu Stadium]]
| FIFA Rank          = {{FIFA World Rankings|SOM}}
| FIFA max           = 65
| FIFA max date      = April–June 1996
| FIFA min           = 203
| FIFA min date      = April–May 2019
| Elo Rank           = {{World Football Elo Ratings|Somalia}}
| Elo max = 126
| Elo max date = 1973
| Elo min = 205
| Elo min date = 2015
| pattern_la1        = _som25h
| pattern_b1         = _som25h
| pattern_ra1        = _som25h
| pattern_sh1        = _som25h
| pattern_so1        = 
| leftarm1           = 73C2FB
| body1              = 73C2FB
| rightarm1          = 73C2FB
| shorts1            = 73C2FB
| socks1             = 73C2FB
| pattern_la2        = _som25a
| pattern_b2         = _som25a
| pattern_ra2        = _som25a
| pattern_sh2        = _som25a
| pattern_so2        = 
| leftarm2           = FFFFFF
| body2              = FFFFFF
| rightarm2          = FFFFFF
| shorts2            = FFFFFF
| socks2             = FFFFFF
| First game         = {{fb|Kenya|colonial}} 0–4 '''Somalia''' {{flagicon image|Flag of Italy and the United Kingdom.png}}<br /> ([[Mombasa]], [[Kenya]]; 1958)<ref name="Mshassan">{{cite book|last1=Hassan|first1=Mohammed Sheik|title=History of Somali football (1938–2007)|date=2008|publisher=Scansom|page=20|isbn=9789185945986 |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=m5MaAQAAMAAJ|access-date=10 July 2014}}</ref>
| Largest win        = {{fb|SOM}} 5–2 {{fb-rt|MTN|1959}} <br /> ([[Casablanca]], [[Morocco]]; 7 August 1985)
| Largest loss       = {{fb|PRK|1948}} 14–0 {{fb-rt|SOM}} <br /> ([[Jakarta]], [[Indonesia]]; 14 November 1963)
| Regional name         = [[CECAFA Cup]]
| Regional cup apps     = 26
| Regional cup first    = [[1973 CECAFA Cup|1973]]
| Regional cup best     = Fifth place ([[1974 CECAFA Cup|1974]], [[1977 CECAFA Cup|1977]], [[1978 CECAFA Cup|1978]], [[1984 CECAFA Cup|1984]], [[1994 CECAFA Cup|1994]], [[2019 CECAFA Cup|2019]])
| 2ndRegional name      = [[Arab Games]]
| 2ndRegional cup apps  = 1
| 2ndRegional cup first = [[Football at the 1985 Arab Games|1985]]
| 2ndRegional cup best  = Group stage ([[Football at the 1985 Arab Games|1985]])
|pattern_la3=_som25t|pattern_b3=_som25t|pattern_ra3=_som25t|pattern_sh3=_som25t|socks3=ffffff|shorts3=ffffff|rightarm3=ffffff|body3=ffffff|leftarm3=ffffff}}
The '''Somalia national football team''' ({{langx|so|Kooxda Qaranka Soomaaliya}}) represents [[Somalia]] in international [[Association football|football]] and is controlled by the [[Somali Football Federation]] (SFF), a member of the [[Confederation of African Football]] (CAF). Somalia's first national team captain was [[Mohamed Shangole]], while its longest serving captain has been [[Hasan Babay]].

==History==
The first Somali football teams were established in the 1940s. The competitions were basic in structure and were associated with the anti-colonial movement. The [[Somali Youth League]] (SYL), the nation's first political party, had put together a team of local youth to play against the Italian expatriate teams. The soccer team the FYL had assembled, which would later change its name to Bondhere, won the first several competitions. In 1951, the [[Somali Football Federation]] (SFF) was founded. The first Somali commissioner for sport was later established in 1958.

Although the Somali national football team took part in preliminary matches, it has never qualified for the [[FIFA World Cup|World Cup]]. For many years after the outbreak of the [[Somali Civil War|civil war]] in the early 1990s, FIFA-sanctioned games could not be played within the country. Qualifying matches for the [[Africa Cup of Nations]] and the World Cup were instead contested away from home. However, following the pacification of the capital [[Mogadishu]] in 2011, the SFF began preparations for the first major sporting event to be held in years at the [[Mogadishu Stadium]], in December 2012.

Prior to 2019, the Somali FA decided they would admit to all their young and upcoming youth talents and put out local trials to fortify both Olympic and national football teams.

In addition, many from the Somali diaspora in the past have produced quality football players such as [[Islam Feruz]] and [[Mukhtar Ali]].

On 5 September 2019, Somalia won their first qualifying match since 1984 (against [[Kenya national football team|Kenya]] - AFCON Qualifying) and their first-ever FIFA World Cup qualifying match, beating [[Zimbabwe national football team|Zimbabwe]] by 1–0.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/football/49621822|title=World Cup qualifying: Somalia national team's history-making driving instructor|website=BBC News|date=8 September 2019|access-date=8 September 2019|archive-date=9 September 2019|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190909055353/https://www.bbc.com/sport/football/49621822|url-status=live}}</ref> They nearly advanced to the second round but lost 3–1 in Zimbabwe after two late goals for the warriors, consigning the Ocean Stars to an early exit.

==Team image==
=== Kits and crest ===
{| class="wikitable"
|-
!Kit provider
!Period
|-
|{{Flagicon|SOM}} A2Z Sports
|2020–present
|}

From the 1970s up until the 1990s, the Somali national football team used to wear the Somali Flag and socks with the Somali Flag for home kits and the inverse as away kits,<ref>{{Cite web|title=Somalia vs Yemen PDR for Arab Cup Qualification 1985 (Arabic)|website=[[YouTube]]|date=2 December 2013 |url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=IXpoRVtMvcU|access-date=10 February 2022|archive-date=21 April 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220421052707/https://www.youtube.com/watch?app=desktop&v=IXpoRVtMvcU|url-status=live}}</ref> the two primary colours of the Somali national flags, in the 2010s up until the present, the national team have now replaced the solid sky blue shirts for vertically blue and white striped shirts.

The crest of the Somali national team used to be the [[coat of arms of Somalia]] but it is now replaced with the [[Somali Football Federation|Somali Federation]] crest. The Ocean Stars plays their home games in the [[Mogadishu Stadium]], (As the stadium was being rebuilt, the national team played matches at Engineer Yaarisow Stadium, which is Mogadishu's secondary stadium for sporting events).

{|style="margin:1em auto;"
|{{Football kit box
| pattern_b =
| leftarm = 4189DD
| body = 4189DD
| rightarm = 4189DD
| shorts = FFFFFF
| socks = 4189DD
| title = Somalia's first colours in the 1970s–1990s
}}
|{{Football kit box
| pattern_b =
| leftarm = FFFFFF
| body = FFFFFF
| rightarm = FFFFFF
| shorts = 4189DD
| socks = FFFFFF
| title = Somalia's second colours in the 1970s–1990s}}
|}

==Results and fixtures==
{{main|Somalia national football team results}}
The following is a list of match results in the last 12 months, as well as any future matches that have been scheduled.

{{legend2|#CCFFCC|Win|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFFFCC|Draw|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFCCCC|Loss|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFFFFF|Fixture|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}

===2025===
{{footballbox collapsible
| round      = [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – CAF Group G#SOM v GUI|2026 FIFA World Cup qualification]]
| date       = 5 September
| time       = {{UTZ|15:00|3}}
| team1      = {{fb-rt|SOM}}
| score      = 0–3
| team2      = {{fb|GUI}}
| goals1     = 
| goals2     = 
*[[Serhou Guirassy|Guirassy]] {{goal|22}}
*[[Faisal Othman|Othman]] {{goal|64|o.g.}}
*[[Ousmane Camara (footballer, born 1998)|O. Camara]] {{goal|90+6}}
| stadium    = [[Mandela National Stadium]]
| location   = [[Kampala]], Uganda
| attendance = 
| referee    = Tanguy Mebiame ([[Gabonese Football Federation|Gabon]])
| report     = https://www.fifa.com/en/match-centre/match/520/288282/288289/400020801
| result     = L
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
| round      = [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – CAF Group G#UGA v SOM|2026 FIFA World Cup qualification]]
| date       = 8 September
| time       = {{UTZ|19:00|3}}
| team1      = {{fb-rt|UGA}}
| score      = 2–0
| team2      = {{fb|SOM}}
| goals1     = 
*[[Allan Okello|Okello]] {{goal|6|pen.}}
*[[Jude Ssemugabi|Ssemugabi]] {{goal|39}}
| goals2     = 
| stadium    = [[Mandela National Stadium]]
| location   = [[Kampala]]
| attendance = 
| referee    = Kalilou Ibrahim Traoré ([[Ivorian Football Federation|Ivory Coast]])
| report     = https://www.fifa.com/en/match-centre/match/520/288282/288289/400020803
| result     = L
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
| round      = [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – CAF Group G#SOM v ALG|2026 FIFA World Cup qualification]]
| date       = 9 October
| time       = {{UTZ|17:00|1}}
| team1      = {{fb-rt|SOM}}
| score      = 0–3
| team2      = {{fb|ALG}}
| goals1     = 
| goals2     = 
*[[Mohamed Amoura|Amoura]] {{goal|7||57}}
*[[Riyad Mahrez|Mahrez]] {{goal|19}}
| stadium    = [[Miloud Hadefi Stadium]]
| location   = [[Oran]], Algeria
| attendance = 
| referee    = Godfrey Nkhakananga ([[Football Association of Malawi|Malawi]])
| report     = https://www.fifa.com/en/match-centre/match/520/288282/288289/400021068
| result     = L
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
| round      = [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – CAF Group G#SOM v MOZ|2026 FIFA World Cup qualification]]
| date       = 14 October
| time       = {{UTZ|17:00|1}}
| team1      = {{fb-rt|SOM}}
| score      = 0–1
| team2      = {{fb|MOZ}}
| goals1     = 
| goals2     = 
*[[Geny Catamo|Catamo]] {{goal|6}}
| stadium    = [[Miloud Hadefi Stadium]]
| location   = [[Oran]], Algeria
| attendance = 
| referee    = Ahmed Abdulrazg ([[Libyan Football Federation|Libya]])
| report     = https://www.fifa.com/en/match-centre/match/520/288282/288289/400021069
| result     = L
}}
{{Football box collapsible
|date= 17 November
|time= {{UTZ|18:30|3}}
|round= [[Exhibition game#Association football|Friendly]]
|score= 1–2
|report= https://www.soccerway.com/match/bahrain-GITLc4Fp/somalia-Aiu9mqIL/?mid=6Ha48bCG
|team1= {{fb-rt|BHR}}
|goals1=
*[[Mohamed Al-Romaihi|Al-Romaihi]] {{Goal|35}}
|team2= {{fb|SOM}}
|goals2=
*[[Mohamed Omar (footballer, born 1999)|Omar]] {{Goal|45}}
*[[Issa Adim Abatari|Marsis]] {{Goal|80}}
|location= [[Riffa]], Bahrain
|stadium= [[Bahrain National Stadium]]
|attendance=
|referee= Saud Al-Samhan ([[Kuwait Football Association|Kuwait]])
|result= W
}}
{{Football box collapsible
|date=26 November
|time={{UTZ|16:00|3}}
|round=[[2025 FIFA Arab Cup qualification]]
|score=0–0
|report=https://www.fifa.com/en/match-centre/match/10013/290556/291903/400020253
|team1={{fb-rt|OMA}}
|goals1=
|team2={{fb|SOM}}
|goals2=
|penaltyscore=4–1
| penalties1 = 
*[[Ali Al-Busaidi|Al-Busaidi]] {{pengoal}}
*[[Nasser Al-Rawahi|Al-Rawahi]] {{pengoal}}
*[[Salaah Al-Yahyaei|Al-Yahyaei]] {{pengoal}}
*[[Mohammed Al-Ghafri|Al-Ghafri]] {{pengoal}}
| penalties2 = 
*{{penmiss}} [[Sak Hassan|S. Hassan]]
*{{pengoal}} [[Mohamed Omar (footballer, born 1999)|M. Omar]]
*{{penmiss}} [[Ali Musse|Musse]]
|location= [[Doha]], Qatar
|stadium= [[Abdullah Bin Khalifa Stadium]]
|attendance= 3,957
|referee= Juan Calderón ([[Costa Rican Football Federation|Costa Rica]])
|result=D
}}

===2026===
{{footballbox collapsible
| round      = [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations qualification preliminary round|2027 Africa Cup of Nations qualification]]
| date       = 27 March
| time       = {{UTZ|15:00|2}}
| team1      = {{fb-rt|SOM}}
| score      = 0–0
| team2      = {{fb|MRI}}
| goals1     = 
| goals2     = 
| stadium    = [[Arena Lalgy Tchumene]]
| location   = [[Matola]], Mozambique
| attendance = 
| referee    = Kouassi Biro ([[Ivorian Football Federation|Ivory Coast]])
| report     = https://globalsportsarchive.com/en/soccer/match/2026-03-27/somalia-vs-mauritius/4048695/overview
| result     = D
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
| round      = [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations qualification preliminary round|2027 Africa Cup of Nations qualification]]
| date       = 31 March
| time       = {{UTZ|19:00|4}}
| team1      = {{fb-rt|MRI}}
| score      = 0–0
| team2      = {{fb|SOM}}
| goals1     = 
| goals2     = 
|penalties1   =
*[[Lindsay Rose|Rose]] {{pengoal}}
*[[Leon Alizart|Alizart]] {{penmiss}}
*[[Jason Ferré|Ferré]] {{pengoal}}
*[[Kevin Perticots|Perticots]] {{penmiss}}
|penaltyscore = 2–4
|penalties2   =
*[[Mohamed Awad (Somali footballer)|Awad]] {{pengoal}}
*[[Ilyas Akram|Akram]] {{pengoal}}
*[[Abdi Sharif|Sharif]] {{pengoal}}
*[[Mukhtar Suleiman|Suleiman]] {{pengoal}}
| stadium    = [[Côte d'Or National Sports Complex]]
| location   = [[Saint Pierre, Mauritius|Saint Pierre]], Mauritius
| attendance = 
| referee    = Ali Brahamou Sadou ([[Nigerien Football Federation|Niger]]) 
| report     = https://globalsportsarchive.com/en/soccer/match/2026-03-27/somalia-vs-mauritius/4048695/overview
| result     = D
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
|round      = [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations qualification|2027 AFCON Qualifiers]]
|date       = TBD
|time       = TBD
|team1      = {{fb-rt|GHA}}
|score      = 
|team2      = {{fb|SOM}}
|report     = 
|goals1     =
|goals2     =
|location   = TBD, Ghana
|stadium    = TBD
|attendance = 
|referee    = 
|result     =
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
|round      = [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations qualification|2027 AFCON Qualifiers]]
|date       = TBD
|time       = TBD
|team1      = {{fb-rt|SOM}}
|score      = 
|team2      = {{fb|GHA}}
|report     = 
|goals1     =
|goals2     =
|location   = TBD, Somalia
|stadium    = TBD
|attendance = 
|referee    = 
|result     =
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
|round      = [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations qualification|2027 AFCON Qualifiers]]
|date       = TBD
|time       = TBD
|team1      = {{fb-rt|SOM}}
|score      = 
|team2      = {{fb|GAM}}
|report     = 
|goals1     =
|goals2     =
|location   = TBD, Somalia
|stadium    = TBD
|attendance = 
|referee    = 
|result     =
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
|round      = [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations qualification|2027 AFCON Qualifiers]]
|date       = TBD
|time       = TBD
|team1      = {{fb-rt|GAM}}
|score      = 
|team2      = {{fb|SOM}}
|report     = 
|goals1     =
|goals2     =
|location   = TBD, Gambia
|stadium    = TBD
|attendance = 
|referee    = 
|result     =
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
|round      = [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations qualification|2027 AFCON Qualifiers]]
|date       = TBD
|time       = TBD
|team1      = {{fb-rt|CIV}}
|score      = 
|team2      = {{fb|SOM}}
|report     = 
|goals1     =
|goals2     =
|location   = TBD, Ivory Coast
|stadium    = TBD
|attendance = 
|referee    = 
|result     =
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
|round      = [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations qualification|2027 AFCON Qualifiers]]
|date       = TBD
|time       = TBD
|team1      = {{fb-rt|SOM}}
|score      = 
|team2      = {{fb|CIV}}
|report     = 
|goals1     =
|goals2     =
|location   = TBD, Somalia
|stadium    = TBD
|attendance = 
|referee    = 
|result     =
}}

==Coaches==
[[File:2015 29 Somali National Team-1 (21007355596).jpg|thumbnail|The Somalia national football team players during a training session in 2015]]
[[File:2015 29 Somali National Team-4 (20410936704).jpg|thumbnail|Another picture of the Somalia national football team players in 2015]]
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;"
!Name
!Nat
!Period
!Matches
!Wins
!Draws
!Losses
!Win rate
|-
|align="left" |[[Qi Wusheng]]
|{{flagicon|CHN}}
|1978–1979
|
|
|
|
|
|-
| align="left" | [[Klaus Ebbighausen]]
| {{flagicon|FRG}}
| 1980
| 
| 
| 
| 
| 
|-
| align="left" | [[Hussein Ali Abdulle]]
| {{flagicon|SOM}}
| May 1999 – Dec 2000
| 8
| 0
| 1
| 7
| 6.3%
|-
| align="left" | [[Awil Ismail Mohamed]]
| {{flagicon|SOM}}
| Nov 2001 – Dec 2002
| 7
| 1
| 2
| 4
| 28.6%
|-
| align="left" | [[Ali Abdi Farah (football manager)|Ali Abdi Farah]]
| {{flagicon|SOM}}
| Oct 2003 – Dec 2005
| 9
| 1
| 0
| 8
| 11.1%
|-
| align="left" | [[Daniel Muwathe]]
| {{flagicon|KEN}}
| Oct 2006 – Dec 2006
| 6
| 0
| 0
| 6
| 0%
|-
| align="left" | [[Hussein Ali Abdulle]]
| {{flagicon|SOM}}
| Oct 2007 – Dec 2007
| 4
| 0
| 0
| 4
| 0%
|-
| align="left" | [[Ali Abdi Farah (football manager)|Ali Abdi Farah]]
| {{flagicon|SOM}}
| Sept 2008 – Dec 2009
| 8
| 2
| 0
| 6
| 25%
|-
| align="left" | [[Mohamed Farayare]]
| {{flagicon|SOM}}
| Jan 2010 – Mar 2010
| 2
| 1
| 0
| 1
| 50%
|-
| align="left" | [[Yousef Adam]]
| {{flagicon|QAT}}
| Oct 2010 – Dec 2010
| 3
| 0
| 0
| 3
| 0%
|-
| align="left" | [[Alfred Imonje]]
| {{flagicon|KEN}}
| Oct 2011&nbsp;– Dec 2011
| 5
| 0
| 1
| 4
| 10%
|-
| align="left" | [[Sam Ssimbwa]]
| {{flagicon|UGA}}
| Dec 2011&nbsp;– Oct 2013
| 6
| 0
| 0
| 6
| 0%
|-
| align="left" | [[Sam Ssimbwa]]
| {{flagicon|UGA}}
| Mar 2014&nbsp;– Sept 2015
| 2
| 0
| 0
| 2
| 0%
|-
| align="left" | [[Charles Livingstone Mbabazi|Charles Mbabazi]]
| {{flagicon|UGA}}
| Sept 2015
| 
| 
| 
| 
| 
|-
| align="left" | [[Haruna Mawa]]
| {{flagicon|UGA}}
| Nov 2016 – Nov 2019
| 
| 
| 
| 
| 
|-
| align="left" | [[Bashir Hayford]]
| {{flagicon|GHA}}
| Mar 2019&nbsp;– 2019
| 7
| 2
| 2
| 3
| 42.8%
|-
| align="left" | [[Said Abdi Haibeh]]
| {{flagicon|SOM}}
| Dec 2019 – May 2021
| 4
| 1
| 2
| 1
| 25%
|-
| align="left" | [[Abdellatif Salef]]
| {{flagicon|MAR}}
| May 2021 – June 2021
| 0
| 0
| 0
| 0
| 0%
|-
| align="left" | [[Salad Farah]]
| {{flagicon|SOM}}
| June 2021 – February 2022
| 2
| 0
| 0
| 2
| 0%
|-
|align="left" | [[Pieter de Jongh]]
| {{flagicon|NED}}
| February 2022 – May 2022
| 2
| 0
| 0
| 2
| 0%
|-
| align="left" | [[Rachid Lousteque]]
| {{flagicon|MAR}}
| July 2022 – 2023
| 0
| 0
| 0
| 0
| 0%
|}

==Players==

===Current squad===
The following players were selected for the [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – CAF Group G|2026 FIFA World Cup qualification]] matches against [[Guinea national football team|Guinea]] and [[Botswana national football team|Botswana]] on 21 and 25 March 2025, respectively.<ref>{{cite web |title=Final Squad |url=https://www.facebook.com/photo/?fbid=1621946635189092&set=a.1186943175356109 |website=Facebook |publisher=Somali Football Federation 1951}}</ref>

''Caps and goals correct as of 25 March 2025, after the match against [[Botswana national football team|Botswana]].''

{{Nat fs g start}}
{{Nat fs g player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Abdirahman Mohamud]]|age={{Bda|2001|1|1|df=y}}|caps=5|goals=0|club=[[Dekedaha FC|Dekedaha]]|clubnat=SOM}}
{{Nat fs g player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Ibrahim Ahmed Hussein]]|age={{Bda|2005|12|23|df=y}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[Waxool FC|Waxool]]|clubnat=SOM}}
{{Nat fs g player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Aleeleya Sheikh Saeed]]|age=|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Mogadishu City Club|Mogadishu City]]|clubnat=SOM}}
{{Nat fs break}}
{{Nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Abel Gigli]]|age={{Bda|1990|8|16|df=y}}|caps=11|goals=1|club=[[SSD Correggese Calcio 1948|Correggese]]|clubnat=ITA}}
{{Nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Mohamud Ali (Somali footballer)|Mohamud Ali]]|age={{Bda|1994|7|8|df=y}}|caps=10|goals=0|club=[[Llandudno F.C.|Llandudno]]|clubnat=WAL}}
{{Nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Yonis Farah]]|age={{Bda|1999|9|4|df=y}}|caps=9|goals=0|club=[[FC Mitrovica]]|clubnat=KOS}}
{{Nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Ayman Mohamed Hussein]]|age={{Bda|2000|12|12|df=y}}|caps=8|goals=0|club=[[Horseed FC|Horseed]]|clubnat=SOM}}
{{Nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Fahad Mohamed|Fahad Fussad]]|age={{Bda|2000|3|21|df=y}}|caps=5|goals=0|club=[[Salon Palloilijat|SalPa]]|clubnat=FIN}}
{{Nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Ali Omar]]|age={{Bda|1999|9|14|df=y}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[Larne F.C.|Larne]]|clubnat=NIR}}
{{Nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Abdulle Abdullahi]]|age={{Bda|2006|11|3|df=y}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[Dekedaha FC|Dekedaha]]|clubnat=SOM}}
{{Nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Suleyman Sidali]]|age={{Bda|2004|10|2|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Dekedaha FC|Dekedaha]]|clubnat=SOM}}
{{Nat fs break}}
{{Nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Ahmed "Banu" Hirabe|Ahmed Hirabe]]|age={{Bda|1997|12|12|df=y}}|caps=12|goals=0|club=[[Horseed FC|Horseed]]|clubnat=SOM}}
{{Nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Abdulsamed Abdullahi]]|age={{Bda|1997|1|19|df=y}}|caps=10|goals=0|club=[[FK Novi Pazar|Novi Pazar]]|clubnat=SRB}}
{{Nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Mukhtar Suleiman]]|age={{Bda|1998|8|10|df=y}}|caps=10|goals=0|club=[[SV Spakenburg]]|clubnat=NED}}
{{Nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Ibrahim Ilyas]]|age={{birth date and age|2000|3|5|df=y}}|caps=6|goals=0|club=[[Kinondoni Municipal Council F.C.|KMC]]|clubnat=TAN}}
{{Nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Aweys Adan]]|age={{Bda|1995|6|14|df=y}}|caps=5|goals=0|club=[[Dekedaha FC|Dekedaha]]|clubnat=SOM}}
{{Nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Mohamed Omar (soccer, born 1999)|Mohamed Omar]]|age={{Bda|1999|1|22|df=y}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[Indy Eleven]]|clubnat=USA}}
{{Nat fs break}}
{{Nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Mohamed Awad (Somali footballer)|Mohamed Awad]]|age={{Birth date and age|1994|5|7|df=y}}|caps=12|goals=1|club=[[Sliema Wanderers F.C.|Sliema Wanderers]]|clubnat=MLT}}
{{Nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Sak Hassan]]|age={{Bda|2001|3|21|df=y}}|caps=11|goals=2|club=[[Wealdstone F.C.|Wealdstone]]|clubnat=ENG}}
{{Nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Yusuf Ahmed]]|age={{Bda|1997|4|24|df=y}}|caps=10|goals=1|club=[[Avondale FC|Avondale]]|clubnat=AUS}}
{{Nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Issa Adim Abatari]]|age={{bda|2002|2|13|df=y}}|caps=6|goals=0|club=[[Mogadishu City Club|Mogadishu City]]|clubnat=SOM}}
{{Nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Ali Musse]]|age={{Bda|1996|1|1|df=y}}|caps=6|goals=0|club=[[Cavalry FC|Cavalry]]|clubnat=CAN}}
{{Nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Mahad Shine]]|age={{Birth date and age|2001|8|19|df=y}}|caps=6|goals=0|club=[[Horseed FC|Horseed]]|clubnat=SOM}}
{{Nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Bilal Habib]]|age={{Birth date and age|2000|11|21|df=y}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[Altona Magic SC|Altona Magic]]|clubnat=AUS}}
{{nat fs end}}

===Recent call-ups===
The following players have also been called up to the Somalia squad within the last twelve months.
{{nat fs r start}}
{{Nat fs r player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Abdi Samad]]|age=|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Waxool FC|Waxool]]|clubnat=SOM|latest=v. {{fb|BOT}}; 10 June 2024}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{Nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Saadiq Elmi]]|age={{Bda|2000|11|11|df=y}}|caps=11|goals=0|club=[[Moss FK|Moss]]|clubnat=NOR|latest=v. {{fb|BOT}}; 10 June 2024}}
{{Nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Abdi Salim]]|age={{Bda|2001|4|1|df=y}}|caps=5|goals=0|club=[[San Antonio FC]]|clubnat=USA|latest=v. {{fb|BOT}}; 10 June 2024}}
{{Nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Isse Abdulkadir]]|age={{Bda|1999|7|1|df=y}}|caps=3|goals=0|club=[[Elman FC|Elman]]|clubnat=SOM|latest=v. {{fb|BOT}}; 10 June 2024}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{Nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Ismail Liban]]|age={{Bda|2001|7|29|df=y}}|caps=4|goals=0|club=[[Bentleigh Greens SC|Bentleigh Greens]]|clubnat=AUS|latest=v. {{fb|BOT}}; 10 June 2024}}
{{Nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Ismael Mohamed|Ismael Shirwa]]|age={{Bda|1994|3|13|df=y}}|caps=4|goals=1|club=[[Werribee City FC|Werribee City]]|clubnat=AUS|latest=v. {{fb|BOT}}; 10 June 2024}}
{{Nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Mohamed Sheik Ahmed]]|age=|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Ohio Dominican Panthers]]|clubnat=USA|latest=v. {{fb|BOT}}; 10 June 2024}}
{{Nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Zakariyah Nur]]|age={{Bda|2005|12|12|df=y}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[Cumberland United FC|Cumberland United]]|clubnat=AUS|latest=v. {{fb|BOT}}; 10 June 2024}}
{{Nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Abdullahi Osman]]|age={{Bda|1999|4|5|df=y}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[Perth SC|Perth]]|clubnat=AUS|latest=v. {{fb|BOT}}; 10 June 2024}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{Nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Yusuf Ali (Somali footballer)|Yusuf Ali]]|age=|caps=1|goals=1|club=[[Vermont Green FC|Vermont Green]]|clubnat=USA|latest=v. {{fb|BOT}}; 10 June 2024}}
{{Nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Handwalla Bwana]]|age={{Bda|1999|6|25|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Las Vegas Lights FC|Las Vegas Lights]]|clubnat=USA|latest=v. {{fb|BOT}}; 10 June 2024}}
{{nat fs end}}

==Player records==
{{Updated|31 March 2026}}<ref>{{cite web |title=Somalia |url=https://www.national-football-teams.com/country/171/Somalia.html |website=National Football Teams}}</ref>
:''Players in '''bold''' are still active with Somalia.''

===Most appearances===

{| class="wikitable sortable" style="text-align: center;"
|-
!width=20px|Rank
!class="unsortable" style="width:190px;"|Name
!width=50px|Caps
!width=50px|Goals
!class="unsortable" style="width:100px;"|Career
|-
|1
|align=left|{{interlanguage link|Omar Abdulkadir|es}}
|31
|0
|1999–2007
|-
|2
|align=left|[[Yasin Egal]]
|20
|0
|2003–2011
|-
|3
|align=left|'''[[Mustaf Yuusuf]]'''
|18
|0
|2015-present
|-
|4
|align=left|'''[[Abel Gigli]]'''
|17
|1
|2019-present
|-
|rowspan=2|5
|align=left|'''[[Abdulsamed Abdullahi]]'''
|16
|0
|2019-present
|-
|align=left|'''[[Mohamud Ali (footballer)|Mohamud Ali]]'''
|16
|0
|2019-present
|-
|rowspan=2|7
|align=left|'''[[Farhan Mohamed Ahmed]]'''
|15
|2
|2019-present
|-
|align=left|'''[[Sak Hassan]]'''
|15
|2
|2022–present
|-
|rowspan=3|9
|align=left|'''[[Mohamed Awad (Somali footballer)|Mohamed Awad]]'''
|14
|1
|2021–present
|-
|align=left|'''[[Ahmed "Banu" Hirabe|Ahmed Hirabe]]'''
|14
|0
|2017–present
|-
|align=left|'''[[Mukhtar Suleiman]]'''
|14
|0
|2022–present
|-
|}

===Top goalscorers===

{|class="wikitable sortable" style="text-align: center;"
|-
!width=20px|Rank
!class="unsortable" width=190px|Name
!width=50px|Goals
!width=50px|Caps
!width=50px|Ratio
!class="unsortable" width=100px|Career
|-
|rowspan=2|1
|align="left"|[[Abdullahi Sheikh]]
|3
|6
|{{#expr:3/6 round 2}}
|2000–2005
|-
|align="left"|[[Mohamed Nur]]
|3
|7
|{{#expr:3/7 round 2}}
|1985–1995
|-
|rowspan=6|3
|align="left"|[[Omar Mohamed]]
|2
|6
|{{#expr:2/6 round 2}}
|2019
|-
|align="left"|[[Mohamed Abdiaziz]]
|2
|11
|{{#expr:2/11 round 2}}
|2006–2011
|-
|align="left"|'''[[Issa Adim Abatari]]'''
|2
|11
|{{#expr:2/11 round 2}}
|2023–present
|-
|align="left"|[[Ciise Abshir]]
|2
|11
|{{#expr:2/11 round 2}}
|2003–2011
|-
|align="left"|'''[[Farhan Mohamed Ahmed]]'''
|2
|15
|{{#expr:2/15 round 2}}
|2019–present
|-
|align="left"|'''[[Sak Hassan]]'''
|2
|15
|{{#expr:2/15 round 2}}
|2022–present
|-
|}

==Competitive record==
{{col-begin}}
{{col-2}}

===FIFA World Cup===

{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
!colspan=9|[[FIFA World Cup]] record
!rowspan=39|
!colspan=6|[[FIFA World Cup qualification|Qualification]] record
|-
!Year
!Round
!Position
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}*
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
|-
|[[1930 FIFA World Cup|1930]] to [[1958 FIFA World Cup|1958]]
|colspan=8|''Part of {{fb|Italy}} & {{flag|United Kingdom}}''
|colspan=6|''Part of {{fb|Italy}} & {{flag|United Kingdom}}''
|-
|{{flagicon|Chile}} [[1962 FIFA World Cup|1962]]
|colspan=8|''Not a FIFA member''
|colspan=6|''Not a FIFA member''
|-
|[[1966 FIFA World Cup|1966]] to [[1978 FIFA World Cup|1978]]
|colspan=8|''Did not enter''
|colspan=6|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|Spain}} [[1982 FIFA World Cup|1982]]
|colspan=8|''Did not qualify''
|2
|0
|2
|0
|1
|1
|-
|[[1986 FIFA World Cup|1986]] to [[1998 FIFA World Cup|1998]]
|colspan=8|''Did not enter''
|colspan=6|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|South Korea|1997}} {{flagicon|Japan}} [[2002 FIFA World Cup|2002]]
|colspan=8 rowspan=7|''Did not qualify''
|2
|0
|0
|2
|0
|6
|-
|{{flagicon|Germany}} [[2006 FIFA World Cup|2006]]
|2
|0
|0
|2
|0
|7
|-
|{{flagicon|South Africa}} [[2010 FIFA World Cup|2010]]
|1
|0
|0
|1
|0
|1
|-
|{{flagicon|Brazil}} [[2014 FIFA World Cup|2014]]
|2
|0
|1
|1
|0
|5
|-
|{{flagicon|Russia}} [[2018 FIFA World Cup|2018]]
|2
|0
|0
|2
|0
|6
|-
|{{flagicon|Qatar}} [[2022 FIFA World Cup|2022]]
|2
|1
|0
|1
|2
|3
|-
|{{flagicon|Canada}} {{flagicon|Mexico}} {{flagicon|United States of America}} [[2026 FIFA World Cup|2026]]
|10
|0
|1
|9
|3
|20
|-
|{{flagicon|Morocco}} {{flagicon|Portugal}} {{flagicon|Spain}} [[2030 FIFA World Cup|2030]]
|colspan=8 rowspan=2|''To be determined''
|colspan=8 rowspan=2|''To be determined''
|-
|{{flagicon|Saudi Arabia}} [[2034 FIFA World Cup|2034]]
|-
!Total
!
!{{Tooltip|0/15|Number of tournaments qualified for}}
!
!
!
!
!
!
!21
!1
!4
!16
!6
!45
|}

===Africa Cup of Nations===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
|-
!colspan=9|[[Africa Cup of Nations]] record
! width="1" rowspan="38" |
!colspan=6|Qualification record
|-
!Year
!Round
!Position
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
|-
|{{flagicon|Sudan|1956}} [[1957 Africa Cup of Nations|1957]]||colspan=8 rowspan=2|''Part of {{fb|Italy}} & {{flag|UK}}''
|colspan=8 rowspan=2|''Part of {{fb|Italy}} & {{flag|UK}}''
|-
|{{flagicon|United Arab Republic}} [[1959 Africa Cup of Nations|1959]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Ethiopia|1897}} [[1962 Africa Cup of Nations|1962]]
|colspan=8 rowspan=4|''Not affiliated to CAF''
|colspan=6 rowspan=4|''Not affiliated to CAF''
|-
|{{flagicon|Ghana}} [[1963 Africa Cup of Nations|1963]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Tunisia|1959}} [[1965 Africa Cup of Nations|1965]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Ethiopia|1897}} [[1968 Africa Cup of Nations|1968]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Sudan|1956}} [[1970 Africa Cup of Nations|1970]]
|colspan=8|''Withdrew''
|colspan=6|''Withdrew''
|-
|{{flagicon|Egypt|1972}} [[1974 Africa Cup of Nations|1974]]||colspan=8 rowspan=2|''Did not qualify''
|2
|1
|0
|1
|2
|5
|-
|{{flagicon|Ethiopia|1975}} [[1976 Africa Cup of Nations|1976]]
|2
|1
|0
|1
|1
|2
|-
|{{flagicon|Ghana}} [[1978 Africa Cup of Nations|1978]]||colspan=8|''Did not enter''
|colspan=6|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|Nigeria}} [[1980 African Cup of Nations|1980]]
|colspan=8|''Withdrew''
|colspan=6|''Withdrew''
|-
|{{flagicon|Libya|1977}} [[1982 African Cup of Nations|1982]]
|colspan=8|''Did not enter''
|colspan=6|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|Ivory Coast}} [[1984 African Cup of Nations|1984]]
|colspan=8 rowspan=3|''Did not qualify''
|1
|0
|0
|1
|0
|1
|-
|{{flagicon|Egypt}} [[1986 African Cup of Nations|1986]]
|2
|1
|0
|1
|1
|1
|-
|{{flagicon|Morocco}} [[1988 African Cup of Nations|1988]]
|2
|0
|1
|1
|0
|5
|-
|{{flagicon|Algeria}} [[1990 African Cup of Nations|1990]]||colspan=8 rowspan=17|''Did not enter''
|colspan=6 rowspan=17|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|Senegal}} [[1992 African Cup of Nations|1992]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Tunisia|1959}} [[1994 African Cup of Nations|1994]]
|-
|{{flagicon|South Africa}} [[1996 African Cup of Nations|1996]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Burkina Faso}} [[1998 African Cup of Nations|1998]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Ghana}} {{flagicon|Nigeria}} [[2000 African Cup of Nations|2000]] 
|-
|{{flagicon|Mali}} [[2002 Africa Cup of Nations|2002]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Tunisia}} [[2004 Africa Cup of Nations|2004]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Egypt}} [[2006 Africa Cup of Nations|2006]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Ghana}} [[2008 Africa Cup of Nations|2008]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Angola}} [[2010 Africa Cup of Nations|2010]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Equatorial Guinea}} {{flagicon|Gabon}} [[2012 Africa Cup of Nations|2012]]
|-
|{{flagicon|South Africa}} [[2013 Africa Cup of Nations|2013]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Equatorial Guinea}} [[2015 Africa Cup of Nations|2015]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Gabon}} [[2017 Africa Cup of Nations|2017]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Egypt}} [[2019 Africa Cup of Nations|2019]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Cameroon}} [[2021 Africa Cup of Nations|2021]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Ivory Coast}} [[2023 Africa Cup of Nations|2023]] ||colspan=8 rowspan=2|''Did not qualify''
|2
|0
|0
|2
|1
|5
|-
|{{flagicon|Morocco}} [[2025 Africa Cup of Nations|2025]]
|2
|0
|0
|2
|2
|5
|-
|{{flagicon|Kenya}} {{flagicon|Tanzania}} {{flagicon|Uganda}} [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations|2027]]||colspan=8 |''To be determined''||colspan=6 |''To be determined''
|-
!Total|| ||0/35|| || || || || ||
!13
!3
!1
!9
!7
!24
|}

===African Nations Championship===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
|-
!colspan=9|[[African Nations Championship]] record
! width="1" rowspan="38" |
!colspan=6|Qualification record
|-
!Year
!Round
!Position
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
|-
|{{flagicon|CIV}} [[2009 African Nations Championship|2009]]||colspan=8|''Did not enter''
|colspan=6|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|SUD}} [[2011 African Nations Championship|2011]]||colspan=8|''Did not qualify''
|2
|1
|0
|1
|1
|6
|-
|{{flagicon|RSA}} [[2014 African Nations Championship|2014]]||colspan=8 rowspan=2|''Did not enter''
|colspan=6 rowspan=2|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|RWA}} [[2016 African Nations Championship|2016]]
|-
|{{flagicon|MAR}} [[2018 African Nations Championship|2018]]||colspan=8 rowspan=3|''Did not qualify''
|2
|0
|0
|2
|1
|4
|-
|{{flagicon|CMR}} [[2020 African Nations Championship|2020]]
|2
|0
|0
|2
|2
|7
|-
|{{flagicon|ALG}} [[2022 African Nations Championship|2022]]
|2
|0
|0
|2
|1
|3
|-
!Total|| ||0/6|| || || || || ||
!8
!1
!0
!7
!5
!20
|}

===Olympic Games===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;font-size:90%;"
|-
!colspan=9|[[Football at the Summer Olympics|Olympic Games]] record
|-
!colspan=9|Appearances: 0
|-
!Year
!Round
!Position
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
|-
|[[1896 Summer Olympics|1896]]–[[Football at the 1960 Summer Olympics|1960]]||colspan=8|''Part of {{fb|Italy}} & {{flag|England}}''
|-
|{{flagicon|Japan|1870}} [[Football at the 1964 Summer Olympics|1964]]||colspan=8 rowspan=7|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|Mexico}} [[Football at the 1968 Summer Olympics|1968]]
|-
|{{flagicon|West Germany}} [[Football at the 1972 Summer Olympics|1972]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Canada}} [[Football at the 1976 Summer Olympics|1976]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Soviet Union}} [[Football at the 1980 Summer Olympics|1980]]
|-
|{{flagicon|United States of America}} [[Football at the 1984 Summer Olympics|1984]]
|-
|{{flagicon|South Korea|1984}} [[Football at the 1988 Summer Olympics|1988]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Spain}} [[Football at the 1992 Summer Olympics|1992]]||colspan=8|''Did not qualify''
|-
|{{flagicon|United States of America}} [[Football at the 1996 Summer Olympics|1996]]||colspan=8 rowspan=2|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|Australia}} [[Football at the 2000 Summer Olympics|2000]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Greece}} [[Football at the 2004 Summer Olympics|2004]]||colspan=8 rowspan=5|''Did not qualify''
|-
|{{flagicon|China}} [[Football at the 2008 Summer Olympics|2008]]
|-
|{{flagicon|United Kingdom}} [[Football at the 2012 Summer Olympics|2012]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Brazil}} [[Football at the 2016 Summer Olympics|2016]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Japan}} [[Football at the 2020 Summer Olympics|2020]]
|-
!Total|| ||0/26|| || || || || ||
|}
*{{small|Football at the Summer Olympics has been an under-23 tournament since the 1992 edition.}}

===All-Africa Games===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
!colspan=9|[[Football at the All-Africa Games|All-Africa Games]] record
|-
!colspan=9|Appearances: 0
|-
!Year
!Round
!Position
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
|-
|{{flagicon|Congo}} [[Football at the 1965 All-Africa Games|1965]]||colspan=8|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|Nigeria}} [[Football at the 1973 All-Africa Games|1973]]||colspan=8 rowspan=2|''Did not qualify''
|-
|{{flagicon|Algeria}} [[Football at the 1978 All-Africa Games|1978]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Kenya}} [[Football at the 1987 All-Africa Games|1987]]||colspan=8 rowspan=8|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|Egypt}} [[Football at the 1991 All-Africa Games|1991]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Zimbabwe}} [[Football at the 1995 All-Africa Games|1995]]
|-
|{{flagicon|South Africa}} [[Football at the 1999 All-Africa Games|1999]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Nigeria}} [[Football at the 2003 All-Africa Games|2003]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Algeria}} [[Football at the 2007 All-Africa Games|2007]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Mozambique}} [[Football at the 2011 All-Africa Games|2011]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Congo}} [[Football at the 2015 All-Africa Games|2015]]
|-
|{{flagicon|MAR}} [[Football at the 2019 African Games|2019]]||colspan=8|''Did not enter''
|-
!Total|| ||0/11|| || || || || ||
|}

* {{small|Prior to the [[1991 All-Africa Games|Cairo 1991]] campaign, the [[Football at the All-Africa Games]] was open to full senior national teams.}}

===CECAFA Cup===
{{Main|CECAFA Cup}}
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
!colspan=9|[[CECAFA Cup]] record
|-
!colspan=9|Appearances: 26
|-
!Year
!Round
!Position
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
|-
|{{flagicon|Uganda}} [[1973 CECAFA Cup|1973]]||rowspan=2|Group stage||6th||2||0||0||2||2||12
|-
|{{flagicon|Tanzania}} [[1974 CECAFA Cup|1974]]||5th||2||0||1||1||2||3
|-
|{{flagicon|Zambia|1964}} [[1975 CECAFA Cup|1975]]||colspan=8|''Did not enter''
|-
|[[File:Flag of Tanzania.svg|border|22px|link=Zanzibar|Zanzibar]] [[1976 CECAFA Cup|1976]]||rowspan=3|Group stage||7th||3||0||0||3||2||7
|-
|style="border: 3px solid red"|{{flagicon|Somalia}} [[1977 CECAFA Cup|1977]]||5th||3||1||1||1||2||2
|-
|{{flagicon|Malawi}} [[1978 CECAFA Cup|1978]]||5th||4||0||1||3||3||10
|-
|{{flagicon|Kenya}} [[1979 CECAFA Cup|1979]]||colspan=8|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|Sudan}} [[1980 CECAFA Cup|1980]]||Group stage||6th||2||0||1||1||1||3
|-
|{{flagicon|Tanzania}} [[1981 CECAFA Cup|1981]]||colspan=8 rowspan=2|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|Uganda}} [[1982 CECAFA Cup|1982]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Kenya}} [[1983 CECAFA Cup|1983]]||rowspan=2|Group stage||7th||3||0||1||2||3||5
|-
|{{flagicon|Uganda}} [[1984 CECAFA Cup|1984]]||5th||3||1||1||1||3||3
|-
|{{flagicon|Zimbabwe}} [[1985 CECAFA Cup|1985]]||colspan=8 rowspan=7|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|Ethiopia|1987}} [[1987 CECAFA Cup|1987]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Malawi}} [[1988 CECAFA Cup|1988]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Kenya}} [[1989 CECAFA Cup|1989]]
|-
|[[File:Flag of Tanzania.svg|border|22px|link=Zanzibar|Zanzibar]] [[1990 CECAFA Cup|1990]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Uganda}} [[1991 CECAFA Cup|1991]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Tanzania}} [[1992 CECAFA Cup|1992]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Kenya}} [[1994 CECAFA Cup|1994]]||rowspan=2|Group stage||5th||3||1||0||2||3||8
|-
|{{flagicon|Uganda}} [[1995 CECAFA Cup|1995]]||8th||3||0||0||3||1||15
|-
|{{flagicon|Sudan}} [[1996 CECAFA Cup|1996]]||colspan=8|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|Rwanda|1962}} [[1999 CECAFA Cup|1999]]||rowspan=4|Group stage||12th||2||0||0||2||0||5
|-
|{{flagicon|Uganda}} [[2000 CECAFA Cup|2000]]||9th||4||0||1||3||1||11
|-
|{{flagicon|Rwanda}} [[2001 CECAFA Cup|2001]]||9th||3||0||1||2||0||6
|-
|{{flagicon|Tanzania}} [[2002 CECAFA Cup|2002]]||8th||4||1||0||3||1||4
|-
|{{flagicon|Sudan}} [[2003 CECAFA Cup|2003]]||colspan=8|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|Ethiopia|1996}} [[2004 CECAFA Cup|2004]]||rowspan=10|Group stage||8th||3||0||0||3||0||7
|-
|{{flagicon|Rwanda}} [[2005 CECAFA Cup|2005]]||7th||4||1||0||3||6||10
|-
|{{flagicon|Ethiopia|1996}} [[2006 CECAFA Cup|2006]]||10th||3||0||0||3||0||8
|-
|{{flagicon|Tanzania}} [[2007 CECAFA Cup|2007]]||10th||3||0||0||3||0||6
|-
|{{flagicon|Uganda}} [[2008 CECAFA Cup|2008]]||9th||4||1||0||3||1||9
|-
|{{flagicon|Kenya}} [[2009 CECAFA Cup|2009]]||10th||3||0||0||3||1||6
|-
|{{flagicon|Tanzania}} [[2010 CECAFA Cup|2010]]||12th||3||0||0||3||0||11
|-
|{{flagicon|Tanzania}} [[2011 CECAFA Cup|2011]]||12th||3||0||0||3||1||11
|-
|{{flagicon|Uganda}} [[2012 CECAFA Cup|2012]]||12th||3||0||0||3||1||13
|-
|{{flagicon|Kenya}} [[2013 CECAFA Cup|2013]]||11th||3||0||0||3||0||7
|-
|{{flagicon|Ethiopia}} [[2014 CECAFA Cup|2014]]||colspan=8|''Not held''
|-
|{{flagicon|Rwanda}} [[2015 CECAFA Cup|2015]]||Group stage||10th||3||0||0||3||0||9
|-
|{{flagicon|Kenya}} [[2017 CECAFA Cup|2017]]||colspan=8|''Withdrew''
|-
|{{flagicon|Uganda}} [[2019 CECAFA Cup|2019]]||Group stage||5th||4||1||2||1||1||2
|-
|{{flagicon|Ethiopia}} [[2021 CECAFA Cup|2021]]||colspan=8|''Did not enter''
|-
!Total||0 titles||26/42||80||7||10||63||35||193
|}

===FIFA Arab Cup===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
|-
!colspan=9|[[FIFA Arab Cup]] record
!width=1% rowspan=39|
!colspan=6|Qualification record
|-
!Year
!Round
!Position
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
|-
|{{flagicon|Lebanon|1943}} [[1963 Arab Nations Cup|1963]]||colspan=8 rowspan=3|''Did not enter''||colspan=7 rowspan=3|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|Kuwait}} [[1964 Arab Nations Cup|1964]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Iraq|1963}} [[1966 Arab Nations Cup|1966]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Saudi Arabia}} [[1985 Arab Nations Cup|1985]]||colspan=8 |''Did not qualify''
|3
|0
|1
|2
|1+
|2+
|-
|{{flagicon|Jordan}} [[1988 Arab Nations Cup|1988]]||colspan=8 rowspan=4|''Did not enter''||colspan=7 rowspan=4|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|Syria|1980}} [[1992 Arab Nations Cup|1992]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Qatar}} [[1998 Arab Nations Cup|1998]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Kuwait}} [[2002 Arab Nations Cup|2002]]
|-
|{{flagicon|}} ''[[2009 Arab Nations Cup|2009]]''||colspan=8|''Did not qualify'' {{Ref|n1|1}}
|3
|0
|0
|3
|3
|18
|-
|{{flagicon|Saudi Arabia}} [[2012 Arab Nations Cup|2012]]||colspan=8|''Did not enter''||colspan=8|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|Qatar}} [[2021 FIFA Arab Cup|2021]]||colspan=8 rowspan=2|''Did not qualify'' 
| 1
| 0 
| 0
| 1
| 1
| 2
|-
|{{flagicon|Qatar}} [[2025 FIFA Arab Cup|2025]]
| 1
| 0 
| 1
| 0
| 0
| 0
|-
!Total|| ||0/9|| || || || || ||
!8
!0
!2
!6
!5+
!22+
|}
{{note|n1|1}} {{small|The [[2009 Arab Nations Cup|2009]] edition was cancelled during qualification.}}

===Arab Games===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
|-
!colspan=9|[[Football at the Arab Games|Arab Games]] record
|-
!colspan=9|Appearances: 1
|-
!Year
!Round
!Position
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
|-
|{{flagicon|Egypt|1922}} [[Football at the 1953 Arab Games|1953]]||colspan=8 rowspan=2|''Part of {{fb|Italy}} & {{flag|England}}''
|-
|{{flagicon|Lebanon|1943}} [[Football at the 1957 Arab Games|1957]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Morocco}} [[Football at the 1961 Arab Games|1961]]||colspan=8 rowspan=3|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|UAR}} [[Football at the 1965 Arab Games|1965]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Syria|1972}} [[Football at the 1976 Arab Games|1976]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Morocco}} [[Football at the 1985 Arab Games|1985]]||Group stage||7th||3||1||0||2||5||6
|-
|{{flagicon|Syria|1980}} [[Football at the 1992 Arab Games|1992]]||colspan=8 rowspan=3|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|Lebanon}} [[Football at the 1997 Arab Games|1997]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Jordan}} [[Football at the 1999 Arab Games|1999]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Algeria}} ''2004''||colspan=8|''Not held''
|-
|{{flagicon|Egypt}} [[Football at the 2007 Arab Games|2007]]||colspan=8|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|Qatar}} [[Football at the 2011 Arab Games|2011]]||colspan=8|''Withdrew''
|-
!Total||Group stage||1/11||3||1||0||2||5||6
|}

===Minor tournaments===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
!colspan=9|Minor tournaments record
|-
!Year
!Round
!Position
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
|-
|{{flagicon|IDN}} [[GANEFO#1st GANEFO|1963 GANEFO]]||Group stage||11th||2||0||0||2||0||23
|-
|{{flagicon|ETH|1897}} 1969 Friendship Cup||Group stage||6th||2||0||0||2||1||14
|-
!Total||colspan=2|0 Titles||4||0||0||4||1||37
|}
{{col-end}}

==See also==
*[[Somali national beach soccer team]]
*[[Somali League]]
*[[Somali Cup]]
*[[Somali Football Federation]]

==References==
{{Reflist}}

==External links==
{{Commons category}}
* {{usurped|1=[https://web.archive.org/web/20110316070219/http://www.somsoccer.com/english/news_center.php Somali Football Federation]}} (archived)
* [https://www.cafonline.com/member-associations/som/ Somalia] at [[Confederation of African Football|CAF]]
* [https://www.fifa.com/about-fifa/associations/SOM Somalia] at [[FIFA]]
* [https://www.rsssf.org/tabless/som-intres.html List of international matches at RSSSF]

{{Football in Somalia}}
{{UAFA Football}}
{{CAF teams}}
{{National sports teams of Somalia}}

{{DEFAULTSORT:Somalia National Football Team}}
[[Category:Somalia national football team| ]]
[[Category:African national association football teams]]
[[Category:Football in Somalia]]
